rationale: The citing paper uses Ailawadi et al. (2006) as part of the core justification for its traffic-lift mechanism, stating that promotion research shows stores run promotions to increase traffic. That mechanism is central to the model's profit logic (sales of nonfocal items generated by traffic), not just peripheral background. Although the citation appears in a short literature block, its functional role is clearly to support a key behavioral/empirical premise used throughout the analysis.
relation_description: Moon and Shugan rely on Ailawadi et al. as empirical grounding for the claim that promotions can increase store traffic and associated sales of other items. This prior evidence helps justify the foundational lift assumption that drives their shortage pricing and purchase-limit model.
